goal: Improve mobile UX for the game view

risks: Touch gesture conflicts: pinch-to-zoom and single-finger pan both need touch events on the canvas. Getting the gesture disambiguation right (tap vs pan vs pinch) without breaking desktop click-to-select is the hardest part.
iOS Safari quirks: iOS lacks requestFullscreen, has inconsistent touch event behavior, and viewport-fit=cover interacts with safe-area-inset-* in non-obvious ways.
Layout integration regression: Replacing the floating bottom sheet with an inline panel changes the entire page layout flow. Must not regress desktop layout while fixing mobile.
over_engineering_flags: CTO flagged 'split view animation' -- simplify to CSS transitions only, no spring physics.
No gesture library needed -- raw touch events sufficient for 3 gesture types.
challenges.recommendation: Proceed as-is. Simplify layout animation to CSS transitions. Skip gesture library -- raw touch events sufficient.

projects.title: Mobile Game UX — Touch Gestures, Layout Integration, and Polish
projects.scope_summary: Fix all mobile UX issues in the game view. Workstream 1: Add pinch-to-zoom and single-finger pan to CanvasOffice.tsx, fix broken zoom bounds math, remove touch-none CSS. Workstream 2: Replace floating bottom-sheet SidePanel with inline panel integrated into page layout (same height as game, scrollable), using CSS transitions. Workstream 3: Collapse header buttons to icons on mobile, add viewport-fit=cover meta tag, shrink AgentTicker, hide fullscreen on iOS Safari, clean up dead code, consolidate zoom constants.
